subject: Ecology
title: Krakatoa: Recolonization in Real Time
description: A quick win: understand Krakatoa: Recolonization in Real Time.

hook.text: When Krakatoa erupted in 1883, it sterilized every nearby island—a blank ecological slate.

buildup.text: Within months, spiders arrived on wind. Within years, grasses and ferns took hold.

discovery.text: By 1930, over 270 plant species and dozens of animal species had colonized the islands.

twist.text: A new volcanic island, Anak Krakatau, emerged in 1927 and began its own colonization story.

climax.text: Krakatoa remains the most studied natural experiment in island recolonization.

punchline.text: Destroy everything and life returns on schedule.

quiz.question: What were the first colonizers of Krakatoa after the 1883 eruption?
